Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Customs Act, the Railway Safety Act, the Transportation of Dangerous Goods Act, 1992, the Marine Transportation Security Act, the Canada Transportation Act and the Canada Marine Act and to make a consequential amendment to another Act
Identification.ShortTitle: Strengthening the Port System and Railway Safety in Canada Act
Identification.RunningHead: Strengthening the Port System and Railway Safety in Canada Act

Stages.StageText: Reprinted as amended by the Standing Committee on Transport, Infrastructure and Communities as a working copy for the use of the House of Commons at Report Stage and as reported to the House on FebruaryÂ 7, 2024

Provision.Text: This enactment amends several Acts in order to strengthen the port system and railway safety in Canada.

The enactment amends the Customs Act to require that, on request, any person in possession or control of imported goods make those goods available for examination in accordance with regulations and deliver those goods, or cause them to be delivered, to a secure area that meets the requirements set out in regulation.
The enactment also amends the Railway Safety Act to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: add a definition of âsafetyâ that includes the concept of security;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: prohibit interference with any railway work, railway equipment or railway operation, or damage or destruction of any railway work or railway equipment, without lawful excuse, in a manner that threatens the safety of railway operations;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: prohibit behaviour that endangers or risks endangering the safety of a station, railway equipment or individuals who are at the station or on board the railway equipment and unruly behaviour toward employees, agents or mandataries of a company;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: authorize the Minister to order a company to take necessary corrective measures if the Minister believes that

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: a measure taken by the company in relation to a requirement of a regulation made under subsection 18(2.1) has deficiencies that risk compromising the security of railway transportation,

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: the security management system developed by the company has deficiencies that risk compromising railway security, or

Provision.Label: (iii)
Provision.Text: the implementation of the companyâs security management system has deficiencies that risk compromising railway security;

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: authorize the Minister to grant, refuse to grant, suspend or cancel a transportation security clearance;

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: strengthen the administrative monetary penalty regime; and

Provision.Label: (g)
Provision.Text: require a review of the operation of the Act every five years.
The enactment also amends the Transportation of Dangerous Goods Act, 1992 to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: require persons who import, offer for transport, handle or transport dangerous goods to register with the Minister;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: provide to the Minister powers relating to the management of safety risks; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: establish an administrative monetary penalty regime.
The enactment also amends the Marine Transportation Security Act to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: set out the Actâs purpose and allow the Minister of Transport to enter into agreements with organizations in respect of the administration and enforcement of the Act;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: set out regulation-making powers that include powers respecting threats and risks to the health of persons involved in the marine transportation system, the sharing of information and the establishment of vessel exclusion zones;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: authorize the Minister to make interim orders and give emergency directions and modify the Ministerâs power to give directions to vessels; and

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: create new offences, increase certain penalties and extend the application of certain offences and the administrative monetary penalty regime to vessels.
The enactment also amends the Canada Transportation Act to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: specify that the Minister may use electronic systems in making decisions or determinations under an Act of Parliament that the Minister administers or enforces and provide that a power of entry into a place under such an Act may be exercised remotely by means of telecommunications; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: reduce the threshold above which the Minister and the Commissioner of Competition must receive notice of proposed transactions relating to a port.
The enactment also amends the Canada Marine Act to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: set out that port authorities are responsible for management of traffic and create regulatory authorities respecting fees and information and data sharing in respect of that management;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: provide the minister with the power to require, by order, the taking of measures to prevent imminent harm to national security, national economic security, or competition; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: require port authorities to establish advisory committees, which must include representatives from local Indigenous communities, require periodic assessments of port authoritiesâ governance practices and set out new requirements respecting plans and reports relating to climate change.
Finally, it makes a consequential amendment to the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada Act.

Heading.TitleText: An Act to amend the Customs Act, the Railway Safety Act, the Transportation of Dangerous Goods Act, 1992, the Marine Transportation Security Act, the Canada Transportation Act and the Canada Marine Act and to make a consequential amendment to another Act

Text.XRefExternal: Strengthening the Port System and Railway Safety in Canada Act

Heading.TitleText: Consequential Amendment to the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada Act

Provision.Text: His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Strengthening the Port System and Railway Safety in Canada Act.
The Customs Act is amended by adding the following after section 19:

Subsection.Text: Any person who has goods in their possession or control that have been imported must, at the request of an officer and in accordance with the regulations, make those goods available for examination, including by delivering the goods, or causing them to be delivered, to a place specified by the officer.
The Governor in Council may make regulations for the purposes of subsection (1), including regulations respecting the time and manner in which to make goods available for examination.

Any person who has goods in their possession or control that have been imported must, at the request of an officer and in accordance with the regulations, deliver the goods, or cause them to be delivered, to a secure area that consists of a building, accommodation or other facility â that is determined to be adequate under a regulation made under subsection 6(3) â for the detention and examination of those goods by customs officers.
The Governor in Council may make regulations for the purposes of subsection (1), including regulations respecting the time and manner of delivery of goods to a secure area.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 3(a) to (d) of the Railway Safety Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: promote and provide for the safety of the public and personnel, and the protection of property and the environment, in railway operations;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: encourage the collaboration and participation of interested parties in improving railway safety;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: recognize the responsibility of companies to demonstrate, by using safety management systems, security management systems and other means at their disposal, that they continuously manage risks related to safety and security matters; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: facilitate a modern, flexible and efficient regulatory scheme that will ensure the continuing enhancement of railway safety.

Section.Text: The portion of section 3.1 of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

The Minister is responsible for the development and regulation of matters to which this Act applies, including railway safety, and for the supervision of all matters connected with railways and, in the discharge of those responsibilities, the Minister may, among other things,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: promote railway safety by means that the Minister considers appropriate;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs (c) and (d) of the definition security document in subsection 4(1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: an order referred to in section 32 or 32.01 that relates to security,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an emergency directive made under section 33 that relates to security,

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a security measure formulated under subsection 39.1(1), and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a requirement or authorization, contained in a notice, referred to in subsection 39.1(2); (texte relatif Ã la sÃ»retÃ© du transport ferroviaire)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 4(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: safetyâincludes security, except in the case of a safety management system or anything relating to such a system; (sÃ©curitÃ©)

security management systemâmeans a formal framework for integrating security into day-to-day railway operations and may include security policy, risk assessments, responsibilities and authorities within the company, security plans, and monitoring and evaluation processes to which the company is subject;â(systÃ¨me de gestion de la sÃ»retÃ©)

Section.Text: Paragraph 6.1(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: railway safety and the safety aspects of railway crossings; or

Section.Text: Subsection 19(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, in deciding whether to approve rules filed by a company, engage any person or organization having expertise in matters relating to safe railway operations to furnish advice in relation to the matter or consult with any other relevant party.
Section 22 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):
A company that applies for an exemption under subsection (4) shall submit to the Minister any document or information specified by the Minister.
Section 22 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (7):
However, the periods referred to in subsection (7) are suspended until the Minister has received any information or document required under subsection (4.1).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Renewals, cancellations or amendments of exemptions
Subsection.Text: The Minister may, in respect of an exemption granted under subsection (2),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: renew the exemption, if in the opinion of the Minister the renewal is in the public interest and is not likely to threaten safe railway operations;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: cancel the exemption, if in the opinion of the Minister the cancellation is in the public interest or the exemption is likely to threaten safe railway operations; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: amend the exemption, if in the opinion of the Minister the amendment is in the public interest and is not likely to threaten safe railway operations.

Subsection.Text: Any exemption granted under subsection (1) or (2) shall be published on the Transport Canada website and by any other means that the Minister considers appropriate within 30 days after it comes into force.
Section 22.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):
A company that seeks an exemption under subsection (1) shall submit to the Minister any document or information specified by the Minister.
Section 22.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):
However, the periods referred to in subsection (4) are suspended until the Minister has received any information or document required under subsection (2.1).
Section 22.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (5):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Renewals, cancellations or amendments of exemptions
Subsection.Text: The Minister may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: renew the exemption, if in the opinion of the Minister the renewal is in the public interest and is not likely to threaten safety;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: cancel the exemption, if in the opinion of the Minister the cancellation is in the public interest or the exemption is likely to threaten safety; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: amend the exemption, if in the opinion of the Minister the amendment is in the public interest and is not likely to threaten safety.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 26.2:

No person shall, without lawful excuse, interfere with any railway work, railway equipment or railway operation, or damage or destroy any railway work or railway equipment, in a manner that threatens the safety of railway operations.

No person shall behave in a manner that endangers or risks endangering the safety of a station, railway equipment or individuals who are at the station or on board the railway equipment, or behave in an unruly manner toward employees or agents or mandataries of a company.
The heading before section 31 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Section 32 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

If the Minister is of the opinion that a company is taking measures in relation to a requirement of a regulation made under subsection 18(2.1) that have deficiencies that risk compromising the security of railway transportation, the Minister, by notice sent to the company,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: shall inform the company of that opinion and of the reasons for it; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: may order the company to take the necessary corrective measures.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 32(3.1) and (3.2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

If the Minister is of the opinion that the safety management system or the security management system established by a company has deficiencies that risk compromising railway safety or railway security, as the case may be, the Minister may, by notice sent to the company, order the company to take the necessary corrective measures.

If the Minister is of the opinion that a company is implementing any part of its safety management system or its security management system in a manner that risks compromising railway safety or railway security, as the case may be, the Minister may, by notice sent to the company, order it to take the necessary corrective measures.
Subsection 32(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

An order contained in a notice under subsection (1) or under any of subsections (2.1) to (3.21) takes effect on the date of receipt of the notice. The notice shall indicate the date, which shall be 30 days after the day on which the notice is sent, on or before which the recipient may file a request for a review of the order and the address to which the request may be filed.

Section.Text: Section 32.3 of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a request for review is filed, an order made under subsection 32(1), (3.1) or (3.2) shall be stayed until the matter is finally disposed of in accordance with section 32.1, 32.2 or 32.4. However, an order made under subsection 32(2.1), (3) or (3.21) or section 32.01 shall not be stayed pending a review under section 32.1, an appeal under section 32.2 or a reconsideration by the Minister under subsection 32.1(5) or 32.2(3).
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 39.2:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, for the purposes of this Act, grant or refuse to grant a transportation security clearance to any person or suspend or cancel a transportation security clearance.
The limitations described in subsection 47.2(2) do not apply to any fees or charges to be paid in relation to transportation security clearances.

Section.Text: Subparagraph 40.1(a)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any rule, standard, order, emergency directive, or security measure made under this Act; and

Section.Text: Section 40.11 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (5):

Subsection.Text: No person shall, while an enforcement officer is carrying out their functions under this Act, fail to comply with any reasonable request of the officer or otherwise obstruct or hinder the officer.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 40.13:

Section.MarginalNote: Assurance of compliance â no notice served

Subsection.Text: If the Minister has reasonable grounds to believe that a person who has not been served with a notice of violation under section 40.14 has committed a violation, the Minister may enter into an assurance of compliance with the person that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: identifies the violation and provides that the person will comply with the provision to which the violation relates within the period and subject to the terms and conditions specified in the assurance;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: sets out the amount and form of any security that, pending compliance with the assurance, must be deposited with the Minister; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: sets out the penalty that the person would have been liable to pay for the violation if the assurance had not been entered into.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may extend the period specified under paragraph (1)(a) if the Minister is satisfied that the person is unable to comply with the assurance of compliance for reasons beyond the personâs control.
A person who enters into an assurance of compliance is deemed to have committed the violation in respect of which the assurance was entered into, unless they request a review under subsection (4).

A person who enters into an assurance of compliance may, within 48 hours after the assurance is signed, unless a notice of default has been served under subsection (6), file a request with the Tribunal for a review of the facts of the violation, in which case the assurance is deemed to be a notice of violation and a review of the facts of the violation and the amount of the penalty is deemed to have been requested under section 40.16.
If the Minister is satisfied that a person has complied with an assurance of compliance, the Minister shall cause a notice to that effect to be served on the person and, on the service of the notice,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: no further proceedings may be taken against the person with respect to the violation in respect of which the assurance was entered into; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any security deposited under paragraph (1)(b) must be returned to the person.

Subsection.Text: If the Minister is of the opinion that a person has not complied with an assurance of compliance, the Minister shall cause a notice of default to be served on the person to the effect that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person is liable to pay, in the time and manner specified in the notice of default, twice the amount of the penalty set out in the assurance without taking into account the limit specified in paragraph 40.1(b); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any security deposited under paragraph (1)(b) is forfeited to His Majesty in right of Canada.

Subsection.Text: The notice of default shall indicate the date, which shall be 30 days after the day on which the notice is served, on or before which a request for a review may be filed and the address where the request may be filed and shall include the particulars concerning the procedure for requesting a review.
On the service of a notice of default, the person served has no right of set-off or compensation against any amount that they spent under the assurance of compliance.
Any security deposited under subsection (1) shall be returned to the person if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a notice is served under subsection (6) and the person pays double the amount of the penalty set out in the assurance of compliance; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a member determines under section 40.18 or an appeal panel decides under section 40.19 that the person has complied with the assurance.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 40.15:

Section.MarginalNote: Request for compliance agreement â notice served
Section.Text: Instead of paying the penalty set out in the notice of violation, the person served with the notice may, within the time and in the manner specified in the notice, request to enter into a compliance agreement with the Minister under section 40.152 that ensures the personâs compliance with the provision to which the violation relates.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, on application made under section 40.151, enter into a compliance agreement with a person who has been served with a notice of violation on any terms and conditions that are satisfactory to the Minister. The terms and conditions may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: include a provision for the deposit of reasonable security, in a form and amount satisfactory to the Minister, as a guarantee that the person will comply with the compliance agreement; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: provide for the reduction, in whole or in part, of the penalty for the violation.

Subsection.Text: A person who enters into a compliance agreement with the Minister under subsection (1) is deemed to have committed the violation in respect of which the agreement was entered into.
If the Minister is satisfied that a person has complied with a compliance agreement, the Minister shall cause a notice to that effect to be served on the person, at which time

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the proceedings commenced in respect of the violation are ended; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any security deposited under paragraph (1)(a) shall be returned to the person.

Subsection.Text: If the Minister is of the opinion that a person has not complied with a compliance agreement, the Minister shall cause a notice of default to be served on the person to the effect that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: instead of being liable to pay the amount of the penalty set out in the notice of violation, the person is liable to pay, in the time and manner specified in the notice of violation, twice the amount of that penalty, without taking into account the limits specified in paragraph 40.1(b); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any security deposited under paragraph (1)(a) is forfeited to His Majesty in right of Canada.

Subsection.Text: On the service of a notice of default, the person served has no right of set-off or compensation against any amount that they spent under the compliance agreement and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person is liable to pay the amount set out in the notice of default in the time and manner specified in the notice of violation; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the notice provides for the forfeiture of the security deposited under the compliance agreement, that security is forfeited to His Majesty in right of Canada and the proceedings commenced in respect of the violation are ended.

Subsection.Text: If a person served with a notice of default pays the amount set out in the notice of default in the time and manner specified in the notice of violation, the Minister shall accept the amount as and in complete satisfaction of the amount of the penalty for the violation and no further proceedings under this Act shall be taken against the person in respect of that violation.

Section.MarginalNote: Refusal to enter into compliance agreement

Subsection.Text: If the Minister refuses to enter into a compliance agreement requested under section 40.151, the person who made the request may, in the time specified by the Minister and manner specified in the notice of violation,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: pay the amount of the penalty set out in the notice of violation; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: file a request for a review under subsection 40.16(1).

Subsection.Text: If a person pays the amount set out in the notice of violation in the time specified by the Minister and manner specified in the notice of violation, the Minister shall accept the amount as and in complete satisfaction of the amount of the penalty for the violation and no further proceedings under this Act shall be taken against the person in respect of that violation.
Section 40.16 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

A person served with a notice of default under subsection 40.131(6) that wishes to have the Ministerâs decision made under that subsection reviewed shall, on or before the date specified in the notice â or within any further time that the Tribunal on application may allow â file a written request for a review with the Tribunal.
Subsection 40.16(2) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
On receipt of a request, the Tribunal shall appoint a time and place for the review and shall notify the Minister and the person that filed the request of the time and place in writing.
Subsection 40.16(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Minister has the burden of establishing that a person has committed a violation or has not complied with the assurance of compliance referred to in the notice of default.
Section 40.16 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (5):
For the purposes of a request for review under subsection (1.1), a person does not have a defence by reason that the person exercised due diligence to comply with the assurance of compliance.

Section.Text: Section 40.18 of the Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (a) and adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that the person has complied with the assurance of compliance, in which case, subject to section 40.19, no further proceedings under this Act shall be taken against the person in respect of the alleged violation; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: that the person has not complied with the assurance of compliance, in which case, the member confirms the decision made by the Minister under subsection 40.131(6).

Section.Text: Subsections 40.19(3) and (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The appeal panel of the Tribunal assigned to hear an appeal may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a determination made under paragraphs 40.18(a) or (b), dismiss the appeal or allow the appeal and, subject to any regulations made under paragraph 40.1(b), substitute its own decision; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a determination made under paragraphs 40.18(c) or (d), dismiss the appeal or allow the appeal and substitute its own decision.

Subsection.Text: After making its decision, the appeal panel shall inform the person and the Minister of its decision without delay and specify the period within which any amount determined by the appeal panel to be payable to the Tribunal is to be paid.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 40.2(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to pay the amount of a penalty set out in a notice of violation, or in a notice of default served under subsection 40.131(6) or 40.152(4), or to file a request for a review under section 40.16;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to pay an amount set out in a decision made under paragraph 40.18(b) or (d) or file an appeal under section 40.19; or

Section.Text: Section 47.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting security management systems.

Section.Text: Paragraph 47.2(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in relation to the filing of documents and the making of applications for, and the issuance of, certificates, exemptions, licences, approvals or transportation security clearances under this Act.

Section.Text: Subsection 51(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister shall, on the fifth anniversary of the day on which this subsection comes into force, and every five years after that anniversary, appoint one or more persons to carry out a comprehensive review of the operation of this Act.
The definition handling in section 2 of the Transportation of Dangerous Goods Act, 1992 is repealed.
The definition safety standard in section 2 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: safety standardâmeans a standard for a means of containment used or intended to be used in importing, offering for transport, handling or transporting dangerous goods, including standards for the means of containmentâs design, manufacture, repair, requalification, equipping, functioning, use or performance;â(norme de sÃ©curitÃ©)

Subsection.Text: The definition prescribed in section 2 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: prescribedâmeans prescribed by regulations;â(Version anglaise seulement)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (b) of the definition safety requirement in section 2 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a requirement for persons engaged in designing, manufacturing, repairing, requalifying or equipping a means of containment used or intended to be used in importing, offering for transport, handling or transporting dangerous goods, or

Subsection.Text: Section 2 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: enforcement officerâmeans a person designated as an enforcement officer under subsection 32.12(1);â(agent de lâautoritÃ©)

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 3:

The Minister may, in writing, authorize any person designated by the Minister to exercise any of the Ministerâs powers and duties under this Act, either generally or as otherwise provided in the instrument of authorization.
Paragraph 5(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person has, subject to the regulations, a registration number assigned under section 6.11;

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: the person complies with all safety requirements and security requirements that apply under the regulations;

Section.Text: Section 5.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

No person shall design, manufacture, repair, requalify or equip a means of containment used or intended to be used in importing, offering for transport, handling or transporting dangerous goods unless the person complies with all safety requirements that apply under the regulations.
Section 5.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

No person shall design, manufacture, repair, requalify or equip a means of containment used or intended to be used in importing, offering for transport, handling or transporting dangerous goods unless the person holds, subject to the regulations, a certificate of registration issued under section 6.2 and complies with all safety requirements that apply under the regulations.
Section 6 of the Act is replaced by the following:

No person shall affix or display on a means of containment a compliance mark that is required or permitted under the regulations â or another mark that is likely to be mistaken for such a mark â in respect of the manufacture, repair or requalification of the means of containment, unless the manufacture, repair or requalification was done in compliance with all safety requirements and safety standards applicable to that compliance mark.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 6.1:

Section.MarginalNote: Application for registration number â dangerous goods

Subsection.Text: An application for a registration number required under paragraph 5(a) shall be made to the Minister in the prescribed form and manner with the prescribed information.
The Minister may assign a registration number to an applicant.
The Minister may collect any personal information or confidential business information that an applicant provides.

Any registration number that was assigned under this Act, on or before the day on which this section comes into force, to a person who imports, offers for transport, handles or transports any dangerous goods is deemed to be a registration number assigned under subsection (2).

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following before the headings before section 7:

Section.MarginalNote: Application for certificate of registration â means of containment

Subsection.Text: An application for a certificate of registration required under section 5.1 shall be made to the Minister in the prescribed form and manner with the prescribed information.
The Minister may issue a certificate of registration to an applicant.
The Minister may amend, suspend or revoke, or impose any terms and conditions in respect of, any certificate of registration.
The Minister may collect any personal information or confidential business information that an applicant provides.
The Minister may disclose or publish any information respecting any certificate of registration, except personal information.

Any registration number assigned, or certificate of registration issued, under this Act to a person who designs, manufactures, repairs, requalifies or equips a means of containment used or intended to be used in importing, offering for transport, handling or transporting dangerous goods, that is valid on the day on which this section comes into force, is deemed to have been issued under subsection (2).

Section.Text: Paragraphs 7.1(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: direct a person with an approved emergency response assistance plan to implement the plan, within a reasonable time as specified in the direction, in order to respond to an actual or anticipated release of dangerous goods to which the plan applies and impose any terms and conditions related to the implementation of the plan that the Minister considers appropriate; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: authorize any person with an approved emergency response assistance plan to implement the plan in order to respond to an actual or anticipated release of dangerous goods.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 7.1:

Subject to the regulations, every person who implements an approved emergency response assistance plan shall report its implementation to the Minister and any prescribed person, in accordance with the regulations, and include in the report any prescribed information.
The portion of subsection 9(3) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notice of defective repair or defective requalification
Subsection.Text: The Minister may direct a person who repaired or requalified a standardized means of containment to issue a notice of defective repair or defective requalification, as the case may be, to the person for whom it was repaired or requalified, or to publish the notice in a manner such that the notice is likely to come to that personâs attention, if the Minister believes on reasonable grounds that the person who repaired or requalified the standardized means of containment

Section.Text: Subsection 14(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A person who carries on any of those activities shall, on request, provide to an inspector or to an enforcement officer the proof that is required under the regulations of financial responsibility.

Section.Text: Paragraph 15(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: means of containment being manufactured, repaired or requalified on which a compliance mark is displayed or will be affixed;

Section.Text: Subsection 17(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If an inspector believes on reasonable grounds that dangerous goods are being imported, offered for transport, handled or transported â or that a standardized means of containment is being sold, offered for sale, delivered, distributed, imported or used â in a way that does not comply with this Act, the inspector may, until satisfied that the activity will be done in compliance with this Act,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: remove to an appropriate place, or direct a person to so remove, the dangerous goods, the means of containment being used to handle or transport them or the standardized means of containment; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: detain, or direct a person to detain, the dangerous goods, the means of containment being used to handle or transport them or the standardized means of containment.

Section.Text: Subsection 18(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to the regulations, any person who has the charge, management or control of a means of containment shall report to every person prescribed for the purposes of this subsection any actual or anticipated release of dangerous goods from the means of containment if the release endangers, or could endanger, public safety.
Paragraph 24(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: information disclosed under sectionÂ 23 and information of a similar nature obtained by an inspector under sectionÂ 15 or by an enforcement officer under section 32.13;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 24(4) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of the administration of this Act, in the case of information referred to in paragraph (1)(b);

Subsection.Text: Subsection 24(4) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: to an enforcement officer for the purpose of training of enforcement officers, in the case of information obtained in a record of a communication referred to in paragraphÂ (1)(b).

Subsection.Text: Subsection 27(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (d.1)
Paragraph.Text: defining, for the purposes of any provision of this Act that relates to dangerous goods, the words âimportâ, âoffer for transportâ, âhandlingâ and âtransportâ and, for the purposes of any provision of this Act that relates to means of containment, the words ârequalifyâ, âsellâ, âoffer for saleâ, âdeliverâ, âdistributeâ, âimportâ and âuseâ;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 27(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (j.2):

Paragraph.Label: (j.3)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the issuance, amendment, suspension, revocation and terms and conditions of a certificate of registration referred to in subsection 6.2(1);

Paragraph.Label: (j.4)
Paragraph.Text: exempting, under any circumstances and conditions specified, any person, class of persons or means of containment from the application of sections 5.1 and 6.2;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 27(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (j.2):

Paragraph.Label: (j.21)
Paragraph.Text: exempting, under any circumstances and conditions specified, any person, class of persons or dangerous goods from the application of section 6.11;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 27(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (k.1):

Paragraph.Label: (k.11)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the form of and manner of making a report referred to in section 7.11 and the circumstances under which the report is required;

Paragraph.Label: (k.12)
Paragraph.Text: exempting any person or class of persons from the application of section 7.11;

Paragraph.Label: (k.13)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the contents and implementation of emergency response assistance plans;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 27(1)(q) and (r) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (q)
Paragraph.Text: exempting any person or class of persons from the application of section 18;

Paragraph.Label: (r)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the manner of making the reports referred to in section 18, the information to be included in them and the circumstances in which they are required;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 27(1) of the Act is amended by striking out âandâ at the end of paragraph (u), by adding âandâ at the end of paragraph (v) and by adding the following after paragraph (v):

Paragraph.Label: (w)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing or providing for anything else that by this Act is to be prescribed or provided for by the regulations.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 27.1(1)(b) to (d) of the Act are repealed.
Paragraph 27.1(1)(j) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the manner of making the reports referred to in section 18, the information to be included in them and the circumstances in which they are required;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 27.1(1) of the Act is amended by adding âandâ at the end of paragraph (k) and by adding the following after paragraph (k):

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing or providing for anything else that by this Act is to be prescribed or provided for by the regulations.

Section.Text: Paragraph 29(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in relation to applying for equivalency certificates described in subsectionÂ 31(1), approvals or registrations.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 31(2) and (2.1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

The Minister may issue an emergency certificate authorizing any activity to be carried on in a manner that does not comply with this Act if the Minister is satisfied that the activity is necessary to deal with an emergency in which there is danger to public safety or to security.
The Minister may, in the public interest or for the purposes of security, issue a temporary certificate authorizing any activity to be carried on in a manner that does not comply with this Act.
Subsection 31(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Minister may revoke an equivalency, emergency or temporary certificate â including an emergency or temporary certificate the contents of which have been issued orally â if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister is no longer satisfied of the matter described in subsectionÂ (1), (2) or (2.1), as the case may be;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the regulations have been amended and have the same effect as the certificate; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister is of the opinion that it is in the public interest to do so.

Section.Text: Subsections 32(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, if satisfied of the conditions described in subsection (2), direct a person engaged in importing, offering for transport, handling or transporting dangerous goods, or supplying or importing standardized means of containment, to cease that activity or to conduct other activities to reduce any danger to public safety or to security.

The Minister may not make the direction unless the Minister is satisfied that the direction is necessary to deal with an emergency that involves danger to public safety or to security and that cannot be effectively dealt with under any other provision of this Act.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 32:
For the purposes of sections 32.11 to 32.28, Tribunal means the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada, established under subsection 2(1) of the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada Act.
The Minister may, by regulation,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: designate as a provision the contravention of which may be proceeded with as a violation in accordance with sections 32.15 to 32.26, any provision of this Act or the regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: prescribe the maximum amount payable for each violation, but not more than

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: $50,000 in the case of an individual, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: $250,000 in the case of an organization;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: establish what constitutes a related series or class of violations; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: prescribe the total maximum amount payable for a related series or class of violations.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may designate persons, or classes of persons, as enforcement officers.

Every enforcement officer shall receive a certificate of designation attesting to their designation and shall, on demand, present the certificate to any person from whom the enforcement officer requests information in the course of the enforcement officerâs duties.

Section.MarginalNote: Power of enforcement officer to enter a place

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of determining whether a violation referred to in section 32.15 has been committed, an enforcement officer may, subject to subsection (2), at any reasonable time, enter any place, including a means of transport, if the enforcement officer believes on reasonable grounds that in or on the place there are

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: dangerous goods being offered for transport, handled or transported;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: means of containment being manufactured, repaired or requalified on which a compliance mark is displayed or will be affixed;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: standardized means of containment;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: books, shipping records, emergency response assistance plans, security plans or other documents that contain information relevant to determining whether a violation referred to in section 32.15 has been committed; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: computer systems, data processing systems or any other electronic devices or media that contain information relevant to determining whether a violation referred to in section 32.15 has been committed, or that have such information available to them.

Subsection.Text: If the place is a dwelling-place, the enforcement officer may enter it without the occupantâs consent only under the authority of a warrant issued under subsection (3).

On ex parte application, a justice as defined in section 2 of the Criminal Code, may issue a warrant authorizing the enforcement officer named in the warrant to enter a dwelling-place, subject to any conditions specified in the warrant, if the justice is satisfied by information on oath that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the conditions for entry described in subsection (1) exist in relation to a dwelling-place;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: entry is necessary for the purpose of determining whether a violation referred to in section 32.15 has been committed; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: entry has been refused or there are reasonable grounds for believing that entry will be refused.

Subsection.Text: The enforcement officer who executes the warrant shall not use force unless the enforcement officer is accompanied by a peace officer and the use of force has been specifically authorized in the warrant.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Power to require attendance of persons
Subsection.Text: An enforcement officer who enters a place may require the attendance of persons whom the enforcement officer considers relevant to the carrying out of the enforcement officerâs functions.
The enforcement officer may be accompanied by any person that the enforcement officer considers relevant to the carrying out of the enforcement officerâs functions.

The enforcement officer and any person accompanying them may enter private property, other than a dwelling-place, and pass through it in order to gain entry to a place referred to in subsection (1). For greater certainty, they are not liable for doing so.
An enforcement officer who enters a place under subsection (1) may exercise the following powers for the purpose of determining whether a violation referred to in section 32.15 has been committed:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: open and inspect, or request the opening and inspection of, any means of containment, including any closures, valves, safety release devices or other appurtenances that are essential to the use of the means of containment to contain dangerous goods, if the enforcement officer believes on reasonable grounds that it is being used to handle or transport dangerous goods or to contain dangerous goods offered for transport;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: open and inspect, or request the opening and inspection of, any means of containment described in paragraph (1)(b) or (c), including any closures, valves, safety release devices or other appurtenances that are essential to the use of the means of containment to contain dangerous goods;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of analysis, take, or request the taking of, a reasonable quantity of anything the enforcement officer believes on reasonable grounds to be dangerous goods;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: examine, or request the examining of, information described in paragraph (1)(d) or (e) that the inspector believes on reasonable grounds is relevant to the purposes of this Act and make, or request the making of, copies of any such information; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: ask questions of any person.

Subsection.Text: If an enforcement officer opens anything, or requests that anything be opened, for inspection or for the taking of a reasonable quantity of anything that is sealed or closed up, the enforcement officer shall provide the person who has the charge, management or control of the thing that was opened with a certificate in prescribed form as proof that it was opened for that purpose.

The person to whom, or for whose benefit, the certificate is provided is not liable, either civilly or criminally, in respect of any act or omission of the enforcement officer in the course of the inspection of the thing or the taking of a reasonable quantity of the thing, but is not otherwise exempt from compliance with this Act and the regulations.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Production of documents, information or electronic data
Subsection.Text: An enforcement officer may, for the purpose of determining whether a violation referred to in section 32.15 has been committed, order a person to provide, on the date, at the time and place and in the manner specified by the enforcement officer, any document, information or electronic data specified by the enforcement officer.

Any person who owns or has charge of a place entered by an enforcement officer under subsection (1) and every person present there shall answer all of the enforcement officerâs reasonable questions related to the functions of the enforcement officer, provide access to all electronic data that the enforcement officer may reasonably require, provide all reasonable assistance in their power to enable the enforcement officer to carry out the enforcement officerâs duties and furnish any information that the enforcement officer reasonably requires for the purpose of determining whether a violation referred to in section 32.15 has been committed.
When an enforcement officer is determining whether a violation referred to in section 32.15 has been committed, no person shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: fail to comply with any reasonable request of the enforcement officer;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly make any false or misleading statement either orally or in writing to the enforcement officer; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: otherwise obstruct or hinder the enforcement officer.

Section.Text: The Minister may establish the form and content of notices of violation.

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes a provision designated under paragraph 32.11(a) commits a violation and is liable to a penalty of not more than the maximum amount prescribed under paragraph 32.11(b) or (d).
The purpose of the penalty is to promote compliance with this Act and not to punish.
A violation that is committed or continued on more than one day constitutes a separate violation for each day on which it is committed or continued.

If a contravention of a provision designated under paragraph 32.11(a) may be proceeded with as a violation or as an offence, proceeding with it in one manner precludes proceeding with it in the other.
For greater certainty, a violation is not an offence and, accordingly, section 126 of the Criminal Code does not apply in respect of a violation.
Due diligence is a defence in a proceeding in respect of a violation.

Section.Text: If an enforcement officer believes on reasonable grounds that a person has committed a violation, the enforcement officer may issue and cause to be served on the person a notice of violation that names the person, identifies the violation and sets out

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the penalty for the violation that the person is liable to pay; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: particulars concerning the time for and manner of paying the penalty and the procedure for requesting a review.

Section.Text: The Minister may establish procedures respecting the service of documents that are required or authorized under sections 32.11 to 32.28, including the manner and proof of service and the circumstances under which documents are deemed to have been served. In the absence of those procedures, documents may be served by personal service or sent to the person, at their latest known address, by registered or certified mail or any electronic means, and a document that is served by registered or certified mail is deemed to be served on the fourth day after the day on which it was mailed.

Subsection.Text: If a person who is served with a notice of violation pays the amount set out in the notice in accordance with the particulars specified in it, the Minister shall accept the amount as and in complete satisfaction of the amount of the penalty for the violation and no further proceedings under this Act shall be taken against the person in respect of that violation.
Instead of paying the penalty set out in a notice of violation, the person may, within the time and in the manner specified in the notice,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: request to enter into a compliance agreement with the Minister for the purpose of ensuring compliance with the provision to which the violation relates; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: request a review of the acts or omissions that constitute the violation or of the amount of the penalty.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Failure to pay penalty deemed violation
Subsection.Text: If a person that is served with a notice of violation does not exercise any right referred to in subsection (2) within the time and in the manner specified in the notice, they are deemed to have committed the violation identified in the notice.
On request made under paragraph 32.18(2)(a), the Minister may enter into a compliance agreement with the person making the request on any conditions that are satisfactory to the Minister, which may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: include a provision for the giving of reasonable security, in a form and amount that are satisfactory to the Minister, as a guarantee that the person will comply with the compliance agreement; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: provide for the reduction, in whole or in part, of the amount of the penalty for the violation.

Subsection.Text: A person who makes a request under paragraph 32.18(2)(a) is, on doing so, deemed to have committed the violation.

If the Minister is of the opinion that a person who has entered into a compliance agreement has complied with it, the Minister shall cause a notice to that effect to be served to the person, at which time any security given by the person under that agreement shall be returned to the person.
If the Minister is of the opinion that a person has not complied with a compliance agreement, the Minister shall cause a notice of default to be served to the person to the effect that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: instead of being liable to pay the amount of the penalty set out in the compliance agreement, the person is liable to pay, in the time and manner specified in the notice of default, twice the amount of the penalty set out in the notice of violation, without taking into account the limits specified in paragraphs 32.11(b) and (d); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the security, if any, given by the person under the compliance agreement is forfeited to His Majesty in right of Canada.

Subsection.Text: Once served with the notice of default, the person may not deduct from the amount set out in the notice of default any amount they spent under the compliance agreement and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person is liable to pay the amount set out in the notice of default in the time and manner specified in the notice of violation; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the notice provides for the forfeiture of the security given under the compliance agreement, that security is forfeited to His Majesty in right of Canada and the proceedings commenced in respect of the violation are ended.

Subsection.Text: If a person pays the amount set out in the notice of default in the time and manner specified in the notice of violation, the proceedings commenced in respect of the violation are ended.

Section.MarginalNote: Refusal to enter into compliance agreement

Subsection.Text: If the Minister refuses to enter into a compliance agreement requested under paragraph 32.18(2)(a), the person who made the request is liable to pay, in the time and manner specified in the notice of violation, the amount of the penalty set out in the notice of violation.
If a person pays the amount referred to in subsection (1), the proceedings commenced in respect of the violation are ended.

A person served with a notice of violation who wishes to have the Ministerâs decision regarding the facts of the alleged violation or the amount of the penalty reviewed shall file a request for review with the Tribunal within 30 days after the day on which the notice of violation is served or within any further time that the Tribunal on application may allow.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Date, time and place for review
Subsection.Text: On receipt of the request, the Tribunal shall appoint a date, time and place for the review and shall notify in writing the Minister and the person who filed the request.

The member of the Tribunal who is assigned to conduct the review shall provide the Minister and the person who filed the request with an opportunity that is consistent with procedural fairness and natural justice to present evidence and make representations.
The Minister has the burden of establishing on a balance of probabilities that a person has committed the violation.
The person who is alleged to have committed a violation is not required to give any evidence or testimony in the matter.

Section.Text: At the conclusion of a review, the member of the Tribunal who conducted the review shall without delay inform the Minister and the person who is alleged to have committed a violation

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that the person has not committed a violation, in which case, subject to section 32.23, no further proceedings under this Act shall be taken against the person in respect of the alleged violation; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that the person has committed a violation and, subject to any regulations made under paragraph 32.11(b) or (d), is liable to pay the amount determined by the member to be payable to the Tribunal by or on behalf of the person and the time within which it must be paid.

Subsection.Text: Within 30 days after a determination is made under section 32.22, the Minister or a person to whom it applies may appeal the determination to the Tribunal.
A party who does not appear at a review hearing is not entitled to appeal the determination, unless the party establishes that there was sufficient reason to justify their absence.
The appeal panel of the Tribunal that is assigned to hear an appeal may dismiss it or allow it and, in allowing the appeal, the panel may substitute its decision for the determination.

If the appeal panel finds that a person has committed a violation, the panel shall immediately inform the person and the Minister of the finding and, subject to any regulations made under paragraph 32.11(b) or (d), of the amount determined by the panel to be payable to the Tribunal by or on behalf of the person in respect of the violation and the time within which it must be paid.
If the appeal panel finds that a person has not committed a violation, the panel shall immediately inform the person and the Minister of the finding.

Section.Text: The Minister may obtain from the appeal panel or member of the Tribunal, as the case may be, a certificate in the form established by the Minister setting out the amount of the penalty required to be paid by a person who fails, within the time required,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to pay the amount of a penalty set out in a notice of violation or to file a request for a review under section 32.21;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to pay an amount determined under paragraph 32.22(b) or to file an appeal under section 32.23;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to pay an amount determined in a compliance agreement, under subsection 32.19(1);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: to pay an amount determined in a notice of default under subsection 32.19(4); or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: to pay an amount determined under subsection 32.23(4).

Subsection.Text: On production in any superior court, a certificate issued under section 32.24 shall be registered in the court and, when so registered, the certificate has the same force and effect, and proceedings may be taken in connection with it, as if it were a judgment in that court obtained by His Majesty in right of Canada against the person who is named in the certificate for a debt of the amount set out in the certificate.

All reasonable costs and charges attendant on the registration of the certificate are recoverable in the same manner as if they had been certified and the certificate had been registered under subsection (1).
An amount received by the Minister or the Tribunal under this section is deemed to be public money within the meaning of the Financial Administration Act.

Section.Text: Proceedings in respect of a violation may not be instituted later than two years after the time when the subject matter of the proceedings arose.
At any time after a notice of violation is issued and served, the Minister may make public the nature of the violation, the name of the person who committed it and the amount of the penalty.

Subsection.Text: Unless the Minister is of the opinion that it is not in the public interest to do so, five years after the day on which the person has paid every penalty that they are liable to pay, the Minister shall remove the notation of a violation committed by that person from the public record of notices of violation.
If the Minister is of the opinion that the removal is not in the public interest, the Minister shall give notice of that fact to the person in writing and provide the grounds for that opinion.
The notice shall include the date that is 30 days after the day on which the notice is served and the particulars concerning the procedure for requesting a review.
The person may, no later than the date specified in the notice or within any further time that the Tribunal on application may allow, file a written request for a review with the Tribunal.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Date, time and place for review
Subsection.Text: On receipt of the request, the Tribunal shall appoint a date, time and place for the review and shall notify in writing the Minister and the person who filed the request.

The member of the Tribunal who is assigned to conduct the review shall provide the Minister and the person who filed the request with an opportunity that is consistent with procedural fairness and natural justice to present evidence and make representations.
The member of the Tribunal may confirm the Ministerâs decision or refer the matter back to the Minister for reconsideration.
The person who requested the review may, within 30 days after the day on which a determination is made under subsection (7), appeal the determination to the Tribunal.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Loss of the right of appeal
Subsection.Text: A person who does not appear at the review hearing is not entitled to appeal the determination, unless the person establishes that there was sufficient reason to justify their absence.
The appeal panel of the Tribunal that is assigned to hear the appeal may dismiss it or refer the matter back to the Minister for reconsideration.
Paragraph 33(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: this Act, other than subsection 32.13(13);

Subsection.Text: Subsection 33(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Every person who commits an offence under subsectionÂ (1),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an individual,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is liable on indictment to imprisonment for a term of not more than three years or a fine of not more than $500,000, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is liable on summary conviction to imprisonment for a term of not more than two years less a day or a fine of not more than $250,000; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an organization,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is liable on indictment to a fine of not more than $10,000,000, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is liable on summary conviction to a fine of not more than $5,000,000.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 34(3) of the Act is repealed.
Paragraph 34(4)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an offence punishable on summary conviction and liable,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of an individual, to a fine of not more than $250,000, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of an organization, to a fine of not more than $5,000,000; or

Subsection.Text: The definitions authorized screening and restricted area in subsection 2(1) of the Marine Transportation Security Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: authorized screeningâmeans anything authorized or required to be done under any regulation, interim order, security measure, security rule or emergency direction for the control, observation, inspection and search of persons or goods to prevent the unauthorized possession or carriage of weapons, explosives and incendiaries on board vessels and at marine facilities;â(contrÃ´le)

restricted areaâmeans an area established under any regulation, interim order, security measure, security rule or emergency direction to which access is restricted to authorized persons;â(zone rÃ©glementÃ©e)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (d) of the definition marine facility in subsection 2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any marine installation or structure, as defined in section 2 of the Oceans Act. (installation maritime)

Section.Text: Paragraph 4(1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: marine installations and structures, in accordance with section 20 of the Oceans Act.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 4:

The purpose of this Act is to promote the security of marine transportation â including the security of people, goods, vessels and marine facilities â in particular by enhancing the resiliency of the marine transportation system, by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: taking measures, including developing a regulatory scheme, that deal with threats and reduce direct and indirect risks to the security of marine transportation and the health of persons involved in the marine transportation system and that prevent the unlawful interference with marine transportation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: harmonizing marine practices and implementing Canadaâs international obligations; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: promoting cooperation with federal, provincial, foreign and international entities and organizations with respect to the security of marine transportation.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may enter into agreements or arrangements for the administration and enforcement of this Act or its regulations and authorize any person or organization, including a port authority incorporated or continued under the Canada Marine Act, a federal Department or agency or a classification society, with whom an agreement or arrangement is entered into to exercise the Ministerâs powers â other than the power to make a regulation, an order, including an interim order, a security measure, a security rule or an emergency direction or the power to grant an exemption under section 12 â or perform the Ministerâs duties or functions under this Act that are specified in the agreement or arrangement.

The Minister must furnish each authorized individual and each individual exercising powers or performing duties or functions for an authorized person or organization with a certificate specifying the powers they may exercise and the duties and functions they may perform and any restrictions or conditions on the exercise of those powers or the performance of those duties and functions.
A holder of a certificate is not personally liable for anything they do or omit to do in good faith under this Act.
Subsection 5(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the establishment of exclusion zones for vessels;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 5(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Regulations â security of marine transportation
Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting threats or direct or indirect risks to the security of marine transportation including the security of people, goods, vessels and marine facilities and to the health of persons involved in the marine transportation system.
The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting fees and charges to be paid in relation to the administration and enforcement of this Act.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Offence relating to regulations â person
Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes a regulation made under subsection (1) or (2) is guilty of an offence and liable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on conviction on indictment

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of an individual, to a fine of not more than $1,000,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than five years, or to both, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a corporation, to a fine of not more than $2,000,000; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on summary conviction

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of an individual, to a fine of not more than $500,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than two years less a day, or to both, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a corporation, to a fine of not more than $1,000,000.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Offence relating to regulations â vessel
Subsection.Text: A vessel that contravenes a regulation made under subsection (1) or (2) is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine of not more than $1,000,000.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 5:
The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting the disclosure of information collected for the purposes of this Act by the Minister to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: departments or agencies of the federal government or members or agents of such departments or agencies; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: departments or agencies of the government of a province or agencies of a municipality or members or agents or mandataries of such departments or agencies.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 6:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may make an interim order that contains any provision that may be contained in a regulation made under this Act, if the Minister believes that immediate action is required to deal with a threat or to reduce a direct or indirect risk to the security of marine transportation or to the health of persons involved in the marine transportation system.
An interim order made under this section has effect from the time that it is made or from any later date that may be specified in the interim order but ceases to have effect on the earliest of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the day on which it is repealed,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the day on which a regulation made under section 5 that has the same effect as the interim order comes into force,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: one year after the effective date of the interim order or any shorter period that may be specified in the interim order, unless the effective period is extended by the Minister or the Governor in Council, and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the day that is specified in the order of the Minister or the Governor in Council, if the Minister or the Governor in Council extends the effective period of the interim order.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may extend the effective period of the interim order for a period of no more than one year after the end of the applicable period referred to in paragraph (2)(c).
The Governor in Council may extend the effective period of the interim order for a period of no more than two years after the end of the applicable period referred to in paragraph (2)(c).
Every person and vessel subject to an interim order must comply with it.
The Statutory Instruments Act does not apply to an interim order. However, the interim order must be published in the Canada Gazette within 23 days after the day on which it is made.

The Minister must ensure that a copy of each interim order is tabled in each House of Parliament within 15 days after the day on which it is made. The copy is to be sent to the Clerk of the House if the House is not sitting.

Section.MarginalNote: Offence relating to interim orders â person

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes subsection 6.1(5) is guilty of an offence and liable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on conviction on indictment

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of an individual, to a fine of not more than $1,000,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than five years, or to both, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a corporation, to a fine of not more than $2,000,000; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on summary conviction

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of an individual, to a fine of not more than $500,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than two years less a day, or to both, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a corporation, to a fine of not more than $1,000,000.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Offence relating to interim orders â vessel
Subsection.Text: A vessel that contravenes subsection 6.1(5) is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine of not more than $1,000,000.

A person or a vessel must not be found guilty of an offence consisting of a contravention of an interim order that, at the time of the alleged contravention, had not been published in the Canada Gazette, unless it is proved that, at the time of the alleged contravention, the person or vessel had been notified of the interim order or reasonable steps had been taken to bring the substance of the interim order to the notice of those persons or vessels likely to be affected by it.

Evidence that an interim order was given to the master or to any person on board who is, or appears to be, in command or charge of the vessel, other than the pilot, is, in the absence of evidence to the contrary, proof that it was given to the vessel.
Subparagraphs 9(a)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of an individual, to a fine of not more than $1,000,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than five years, or to both, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a corporation, to a fine of not more than $2,000,000; or

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 9(b)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of an individual, to a fine of not more than $500,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than two years less a day, or to both, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a corporation, to a fine of not more than $1,000,000.

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 11(a)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of an individual, to a fine of not more than $1,000,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than five years, or to both, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a corporation, to a fine of not more than $2,000,000; or

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 11(b)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of an individual, to a fine of not more than $500,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than two years less a day, or to both, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a corporation, to a fine of not more than $1,000,000.

Section.Text: Section 12 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister may, on any terms and conditions the Minister considers necessary, exempt any person, vessel or marine facility from the application of any regulation, interim order, security measure or security rule if, in the opinion of the Minister, the exemption is in the public interest and is not likely to affect marine security.
Paragraphs 13(2)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an individual, to a fine of not more than $500,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than two years less a day, or to both; or
